note: Pilot confirms tau=0.0 paradox: CD-SSD(tau=0.9) does NOT outperform naive T=16. Step reduction (T=16 vs T=64) drives speedup; accept gate adds no marginal value. M1+naive-T16 Ortho=1.010 (super-multiplicative). Recommended paper revision: reframe CD-SSD as a step-reduction method with confidence-guided token scheduling.

candidates.notes: The simplified pilot reveals: (1) M1 accuracy is fully preserved (GSM8K=0.73 vs baseline=0.73) across all 4 entropy thresholds. (2) Cache hit rates are meaningful (0.58-0.75), indicating that 58-75% of token positions would benefit from KV reuse. (3) No actual TPS speedup is achieved because the simplified implementation still runs full forward passes. Real speedup requires d2Cache integration which skips KV computation for cached tokens. Theoretical speedup estimate: 1/(1-hit_rate) = 1.4x to 4.0x depending on threshold. IGSD pilot (H6) shows accept_rate=0.637 at tau=0.85 → PROCEED with IGSD. Recommended operating point for M1: entropy_threshold=1.0 (hit_rate=0.630, theoretical 2.7x). HumanEval pass@1=0.0 across all settings (baseline also ~0.04, within noise for 50 samples).

pilot_m1_single.key_finding: Cache hit rates 0.58-0.75 indicate real speedup potential; need d2Cache kernel integration

pilot_m2_single.key_finding: M2 achieves real 2x TPS speedup at step_jump=2x (117 TPS vs 58.55 baseline). However accuracy drops significantly: GSM8K from 0.73 to 0.34 at 2x (53% drop), and to 0.10 at 4x, 0.01 at 6x+. All step-jump settings exceed 5% accuracy budget. Critical issue: naive top-k unmasking without backtracking causes token commitment errors. Real Saber implementation uses backtracking to correct early commitments.
pilot_m2_single.warning: Accuracy drop is 53% at 2x - exceeds pilot threshold. Speed is achieved but quality is not maintained. Need to implement Saber's backtracking mechanism or reduce step_jump significantly.

pilot_m3_single.key_finding: M3 AR-guided unmasking with Qwen2.5-0.5B supervisor maintains baseline accuracy (GSM8K=0.730 = baseline) but adds ~12% overhead (0.88x TPS). All 4 guidance weights yield identical accuracy (0.730) — Qwen guidance has no negative impact on quality but provides no speedup. HumanEval pass@1=0 (same as baseline). Vocabulary bridging (LLaDA→text→Qwen tokenizer) adds latency without quality gain. M3 is viable as a quality-preservation layer in composability pairs, not standalone accelerator.
pilot_m3_single.composability_implication: Use guidance_weight=0.7 as M3 operating point for pairwise orthogonality experiments. M3+M2 combination: M2 reduces steps (speedup), M3 maintains quality at reduced steps.

pilot_tau0_comparison.key_finding: naive-T16 outperforms CD-SSD(tau=0.9) — step-reduction is the driver

decisions.interpretation: naive-T16 outperforms CD-SSD(tau=0.9) — step-reduction is the driver

next_steps: M2: Implement Saber backtracking to recover accuracy at step_jump=2x. Or try step_jump=1.5x (only slightly reduce steps) to find accuracy-preserving setting.
M2 critical finding: naive step reduction halves accuracy at 2x jump. Saber uses confidence-based adaptive scheduling with backtracking - this is what enables accuracy preservation.
Integrate d2Cache (dLLMCache) into LLaDA-8B forward pass for true KV-cache speedup for M1
All individual pilots now complete: setup_env, baseline, H6, M1, M2, IGSD. Ready for pairwise orthogonality pilots.

m3_summary.key_limitation: Cross-tokenizer bridging adds overhead; Qwen guidance does not hurt quality
m3_summary.composability_role: Quality-preservation layer; pairs with M2 for accuracy-maintained step reduction
